hooks.prompt:
A pull request was just created.

1. Get PR info: gh pr view --json number,title,body --jq '{number: .number, title: .title, body: .body}'
2. Get changed files: git diff main...HEAD --name-only

If NO files are inside the `frontend/` directory, exit silently.

If ANY files are inside the `frontend/` directory, act as the ui-tester agent and run all frontend tests:
  a. cd frontend && npm run test:run
  b. cd frontend && npx tsc --noEmit
  c. cd frontend && npx playwright test --project=smoke

Extract ticket numbers from the PR body (look for #NNN patterns). Today's date is available via: date '+%Y-%m-%d'

REGARDLESS of outcome, append an entry to `.claude/agents/changelogs/ui-tester.md` (newest-first, after the HTML comment block):
```
## <date> — PR #<number>: <title>
**Ticket(s)**: <ticket numbers from PR body, or "unknown">
**Verdict**: APPROVED ✓  (or BLOCKED ✗)
**Tests**: vitest: pass/fail | tsc: pass/fail | playwright: pass/fail
**Discoveries**: <any flaky tests, missing coverage, notable findings, or "None">
```

If any tests FAIL:
- Post: gh pr comment <number> --body "## UI Tester Report\n**Verdict: BLOCKED** ✗\n\n<test failure details>"
- Mark verdict BLOCKED ✗ in changelog

If all tests PASS:
- Post: gh pr comment <number> --body "## UI Tester Report\n**Verdict: APPROVED** ✓\n\nAll frontend tests passed: vitest ✓ · tsc ✓ · playwright ✓"
- Mark verdict APPROVED ✓ in changelog

A pull request was just created.

1. Get PR info: gh pr view --json number,title,body --jq '{number: .number, title: .title, body: .body}'
2. Get changed files: git diff main...HEAD --name-only

If ALL changed files are inside `frontend/` or `.github/`, exit silently — frontend changes are handled by the UI Tester.

Otherwise (any backend, .claude config, Go code, or other non-frontend changes exist), act as the lead-engineer agent:
  a. For any changed Go module directories: cd <module> && go vet ./... && go test -race ./...
  b. Run gofumpt on any changed .go files and verify no formatting changes are needed
  c. Review git diff main...HEAD against CLAUDE.md rules — flag over-engineering, scope creep, missing tests, or violations

Extract ticket numbers from the PR body (look for #NNN patterns). Today's date is available via: date '+%Y-%m-%d'

REGARDLESS of outcome, append an entry to `.claude/agents/changelogs/lead-engineer.md` (newest-first, after the HTML comment block):
```
## <date> — PR #<number>: <title>
**Ticket(s)**: <ticket numbers from PR body, or "unknown">
**Verdict**: APPROVED ✓  (or BLOCKED ✗)
**Checks**: go vet: pass/fail/skip | go test: pass/fail/skip | gofumpt: clean/dirty/skip | CLAUDE.md: pass/violations
**Discoveries**: <architectural notes, missing tests, scope concerns, or "None">
```

If any issues are found:
- Post: gh pr comment <number> --body "## Lead Engineer Review\n**Verdict: BLOCKED** ✗\n\n<findings>"
- Mark verdict BLOCKED ✗ in changelog

If everything passes:
- Post: gh pr comment <number> --body "## Lead Engineer Review\n**Verdict: APPROVED** ✓\n\nAll checks passed: go vet ✓ · go test ✓ · gofumpt ✓ · CLAUDE.md compliance ✓"
- Mark verdict APPROVED ✓ in changelog

A pull request has just been approved. Run functional tests against the ticket's acceptance criteria.

1. Get PR info: gh pr view --json number,title,body --jq '{number: .number, title: .title, body: .body}'
2. Get changed files: git diff main...HEAD --name-only
3. Extract ticket number(s) from PR body (look for #NNN patterns)
4. For each ticket: gh issue view <number> --json title,body --jq '{title: .title, body: .body}'
   Read the acceptance criteria carefully.

Determine role from changed files:
- Any `frontend/` files → act as ui-tester agent
- Any Go/backend/.claude files → act as lead-engineer agent
- Both → run as both sequentially

Run TARGETED functional tests based on the ticket acceptance criteria:
  FRONTEND: Find and run the most relevant playwright test for the changed feature.
    cd frontend && npx playwright test <relevant-spec-file>
    If no relevant test exists, note the coverage gap.
  BACKEND: Find and run the most relevant Go tests for changed packages.
    cd <module> && go test -v -run <TestName> ./...

Get today's date: date '+%Y-%m-%d'

Append a functional test block to the relevant changelog (`ui-tester.md` or `lead-engineer.md`), nested under the most recent PR entry:
```
### Functional Test — <date>
**Acceptance Criteria**: <bullet list from ticket>
**Result**: PASSED ✓ | FAILED ✗
**Tests Run**: <test names>
**Notes**: <what was verified, or what failed and why>
```

Post a comment on the PR:
  PASS: gh pr comment <number> --body "## Functional Test Report\n**Verdict: PASSED** ✓\n\n**Acceptance Criteria Verified:**\n<bulleted list>\n\n**Tests Run:**\n<test names and results>"
  FAIL: gh pr comment <number> --body "## Functional Test Report\n**Verdict: FAILED** ✗\n\n<what failed and why — include relevant test output>"
hooks.statusMessage: Running functional tests against ticket acceptance criteria...
